titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Lloyd Trufelman Collection on Julius Hemphill MSS.663
titlestmt.author: Rachel Searcy

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-05-14 14:30:58 UTC.

did.unittitle: Lloyd Trufelman Collection on Julius Hemphill
did.abstract: Lloyd Trufelman was active in the Downtown New York City experimental arts scene in the 1970s and 1980s. This collection (dated 1978-1990) consists of materials collected by Trufelman documenting the musical career of jazz composer and saxophone player Julius Hemphill. Materials include clippings, correspondence, and ephemera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection (dated 1978-1990) consists of materials collected by Lloyd Trufelman documenting the musical career of jazz composer and saxophone player Julius Hemphill. Materials include clippings, correspondence, and ephemera.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection has not been arranged by an archivist. The materials are arranged in the order in which they were received from the donor.

bioghist.head: Biographical Note
bioghist.p: Lloyd Trufelman was active in the Downtown New York City experimental arts scene in the 1970s and 1980s.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: This collection is protected by copyright and/or related rights. You are free to use materials in the collection in any way that is permitted by the copyright and related rights legislation that applies to your use. For other uses you need to obtain permission from the rights-holder(s).

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Donated by Lloyd Trufelman in at an unknown date between 2018-2026; the accession number associated with this gift is 2026.028.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item, date; Lloyd Trufelman Collection on Julius Hemphill; MSS 663; box number; folder number or item identifier; Fales Library and Special Collections, New York University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: At the time of accessioning, materials were rehoused in an archival box and folder, and descibed on the collection-level.
